dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9638 IH: Sunshine for Our Kids Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Scanlon (for herself and Mr. Harrigan) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To make standard time permanent except in States that elect to observe daylight saving time, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Sunshine for Our Kids Act of 2026.

subsection.text: Section 3 of the Uniform Time Act of 1966 (15 U.S.C. 260a) is hereby repealed.

subsection.header: Exception if State elects To observe daylight saving time
subsection.text: Section 1 of the Act of March 19, 1918 (15 U.S.C. 261; commonly known as the Calder Act), is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating subsection (b) as subsection (c); and
by inserting after subsection (a) the following:

subsection.header: State election To observe daylight saving time

paragraph.text: A State may by law provide that the standard time for such State shall be advanced by 1 hour (either year-round or for a portion of the year, as specified in such law), but only if such law provides that the entire State (including all political subdivisions thereof) shall observe the same standard time.

paragraph.header: States in more than 1 time zone
paragraph.text: In the case of a State that has areas within more than 1 time zone, such State may by law provide for—

subparagraph.text: the standard time for the entire State to be advanced as described in paragraph (1); or
the standard time for the entire area of such State lying within any time zone to be advanced by 1 hour (either year-round or for a portion of the year, as specified in such law).

paragraph.text: In this subsection, the term State has the meaning given such term in section 7 of the Uniform Time Act of 1966 (15 U.S.C. 267).

subsection.text: The second sentence of section 1(a) of the Act of March 19, 1918 (15 U.S.C. 261(a)), is amended by striking Except as provided in section 3(a) of the Uniform Time Act of 1966 (15 U.S.C. 260a), and inserting Except as provided in subsection (b),.
The amendments made by this section shall take effect on the first Sunday of November that first occurs after the date of the enactment of this Act.
